name: RCLI
description: Talk to your Mac, query your docs, no cloud required. On-device voice AI + RAG

commit.message:
docs: fix brew troubleshooting commands (remove invalid flag, add cache clear)

Made-with: Cursor

fix: update Homebrew formula SHA256 for v0.3.3 release

Made-with: Cursor

chore: bump version to v0.3.3 for release

Includes fixes from #19:
- MetalRT crash guard with verification inference
- package.sh hard-fail on missing dylibs
- Null dereference on MetalRT fallback path
- M5+ forward-compatible chip detection

Made-with: Cursor

fix: MetalRT segfault crashes, missing dylib packaging, and M5+ forward-compatibility (#19)

* refactor: remove all benchmarking code and metrics except TTFA

Remove the entire benchmark subsystem and inline performance metrics
(tok/s, TTFT, prefill, decode, STT ms, TTS ms) while preserving
TTFA (Time To First Audio) tracking throughout the pipeline.

Deleted:
- src/bench/ directory (benchmark.h, benchmark.cpp)
- rcli bench CLI command and all bench arguments
- rcli metalrt bench subcommand
- [B] bench panel and [V] verbose metrics toggle in TUI
- API functions: rcli_benchmark, rcli_run_full_benchmark,
  rcli_get_last_llm_perf, rcli_get_last_llm_perf_extended,
  rcli_get_last_tts_perf, rcli_get_last_stt_perf
- print_llm_perf, print_tts_perf, print_stt_perf helpers

Preserved:
- TTFA tracking and display in TUI models panel
- rcli_get_context_info (context window gauge)
- Voice bench test (TTFA-only) in test_pipeline.cpp

* fix: MetalRT segfault on M3/M4, missing dylib packaging, null engine fallback

Addresses three distinct crash scenarios from issue #5:

1. MetalRT GPU crash guard now covers verification inference. The crash
   guard (SIGSEGV handler + breadcrumb) previously only protected
   metalrt_.init() but the actual Metal GPU compute can segfault on
   certain M3/M4 hardware. Now a verification generate runs inside the
   protected section — if it crashes, the breadcrumb persists and next
   launch falls back to llama.cpp. Also removed the unprotected warm-up
   call from main.cpp.

2. package.sh now fails if libonnxruntime is missing instead of silently
   producing an incomplete tarball. Added a recursive fallback search
   path and post-copy validation that checks every @rpath dylib the
   binary references is present in lib/.

3. Defensive null checks: when MetalRT verification fails, attempts late
   llama.cpp init as fallback. Added null-safety guard in
   rcli_process_command MetalRT path. Final validation catches
   impossible states (MetalRT flagged active but not initialized).

Closes #5


* fix: future-proof Metal 3.1 chip detection for M5+ support

Switch gpu_supports_metal31() from an allowlist (M3, M4) to a denylist
(M1, M2) so future Apple Silicon generations are automatically supported
without requiring a code change each generation.

---------

Co-authored-by: AmanSwar <aman.swar001@gmail.com>

commit.message: fix

fix: force-update Homebrew tap before install to get latest formula

The install script was installing stale versions because `brew tap`
doesn't update an already-tapped repo. Now runs `brew update --force`
and tries `brew upgrade` before `brew install`.

Made-with: Cursor

fix: remove built-in tools from LLM visibility and harden system prompt (#10)

Remove get_current_time and calculate from tool definitions exposed to the
LLM to prevent small models from proactively calling tools on greetings.
The tools remain callable in tool_engine but are no longer advertised.
Also fix stale +2 offset in get_filtered_definitions_json and strengthen
the system prompt to explicitly forbid tool calls for casual conversation.
Bump version to 0.3.1.

fix: remove built-in tools from LLM visibility and harden system prompt (#10)

Remove get_current_time and calculate from tool definitions exposed to the
LLM to prevent small models from proactively calling tools on greetings.
The tools remain callable in tool_engine but are no longer advertised.
Also fix stale +2 offset in get_filtered_definitions_json and strengthen
the system prompt to explicitly forbid tool calls for casual conversation.
Bump version to 0.3.1.

commit.message:
fix: guard against MetalRT init crash and uninitialized LLM segfault

Fixes #5. Addresses segfault on M3/M4 Macs where MetalRT dylib calls
(cache_prompt, whisper_create) crash during initialization.

Three-layer defense:
1. Crash breadcrumb: write a marker file before MetalRT dylib init.
   If the process crashes, the marker persists and the next launch
   automatically skips MetalRT, falling back to llama.cpp.
2. Signal handler: install SIGSEGV/SIGBUS handler during MetalRT init
   that prints a helpful message instead of bare "segmentation fault".
3. Defensive guards: check llm().is_initialized() before accessing
   the LLM profile in rcli_process_command, rcli_process_and_speak,
   and rcli_rag_query. Also validate at least one LLM backend is
   available at the end of orchestrator init.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: display TTFA metric on LLM perf line as soon as first audio fires

TTFA (Time To First Audio) is now appended to the LLM metrics line
immediately when first audio is ready, rather than waiting for TTS to
complete. Handles both event orderings (first_audio before/after response).
TTS metrics remain as their own separate line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add `rcli metalrt` and `rcli llamacpp` as engine-specific launch commands

Allow users to launch the interactive TUI with a specific engine forced
for the session (without changing the persistent preference). Also removes
the in-TUI engine switcher panel since engine selection is now done at
launch time via CLI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: update Homebrew formula to v0.3.0

Made-with: Cursor

fix: MetalRT-only mode no longer requires llama.cpp models

When engine preference is "metalrt", the pipeline now treats llama.cpp
STT/LLM/TTS init failures as warnings instead of fatal errors. This
allows users who only selected MetalRT in setup to run without needing
any GGUF models, Zipformer, or Piper TTS files on disk.

Made-with: Cursor

chore: update Homebrew formula to v0.2.9

Made-with: Cursor

fix: load_preferences replaces enabled set instead of merging

Old actions.json files (from v0.2.5 where all 38 actions were default
enabled) would re-enable all actions on load, overriding the new
default_enabled=false settings. Now load_preferences() clears the
enabled set first and uses the saved file as source of truth.

Made-with: Cursor

docs: add context reset tip for tool calling + X keybinding

Made-with: Cursor

docs: streamline README — MetalRT M3+ warning, cleaner layout

- Add M3+ requirement notice for MetalRT with M1/M2 coming soon
- Position MetalRT as the flagship engine by RunAnywhere
- Remove redundant benchmark tables (was listed twice)
- Consolidate model listings into compact format
- Remove excessive badge clutter
- Cleaner feature sections without repetition

Made-with: Cursor

chore: update Homebrew formula to v0.2.8

Made-with: Cursor

fix: configure MetalRT STT/TTS paths for AUTO engine mode + graceful fallback

Root cause: when engine preference is "auto" (chosen via "Both" in setup)
or empty (fresh install), rcli_api.cpp only set config.llm_backend=AUTO
but never populated MetalRT model/STT/TTS paths. The orchestrator would
then activate MetalRT LLM but hard-fail on missing STT/TTS config.

Fix:
- AUTO mode now scans for installed MetalRT models and configures all
  paths (LLM, STT, TTS) so the orchestrator has them available
- Empty engine preference (fresh install) treated same as AUTO
- Orchestrator: when AUTO mode MetalRT STT/TTS init fails, gracefully
  fall back to llama.cpp instead of hard-failing the entire pipeline

Made-with: Cursor

fix: auto-download missing MetalRT STT/TTS on init, fix dir name mismatch

On fresh installs, MetalRT LLM could initialize but STT/TTS detection
failed because:
1. The STT/TTS auto-download only ran when the LLM model was also missing
2. Hardcoded path check used 'lfm2.5-1.2b-4bit' but actual dir is
   'LFM2.5-1.2B-MLX-4bit'

Now if MetalRT LLM is found but STT or TTS is missing, the init code
auto-downloads the default components before proceeding. Falls back to
llama.cpp gracefully if downloads fail.

Made-with: Cursor

Bump to v0.2.6

Made-with: Cursor

fix: only enable 12 core actions by default, rest are on-demand

Fresh installs had all 38 actions enabled, injecting ~1200 tokens of tool
definitions into every system prompt. This consumed ~30% of the 4096-token
context window, leaving less room for conversation history.

Now only 12 frequently-used actions are enabled by default:
  open_app, create_note, create_reminder, get_battery, get_now_playing,
  get_wifi, next_track, play_on_spotify, play_pause_music, search_web,
  set_volume, toggle_dark_mode

The remaining 26 actions are registered but disabled — users can enable
them via the TUI actions panel or rcli action commands. Existing users
with ~/.rcli/actions.json preferences are unaffected.

This frees ~800 tokens of context for conversation history on fresh installs.

Made-with: Cursor

chore: update Homebrew formula to v0.2.5

Adds M3+ requirement note for MetalRT in caveats.

Made-with: Cursor

fix: block MetalRT on M1/M2 with clear error (requires Apple M3+ for Metal 3.1)

MetalRT shaders use bfloat16 and -std=metal3.1, which requires Apple GPU
Family 9 (M3 or later). M1/M2 chips cannot load the metallib at all.

Added gpu_supported() check to every MetalRT entry point:
- metalrt_loader load()/install(): rejects early with clear message
- rcli setup: auto-selects llama.cpp on M1/M2, hides MetalRT choice
- rcli engine metalrt: prints "requires M3+" error
- rcli metalrt install: same check
- TUI engine switcher: shows "Requires Apple M3 or later" label
- rcli_init: graceful fallback to llama.cpp with warning

Made-with: Cursor

Bump to v0.2.4

Made-with: Cursor

fix: auto-download MetalRT models and engine during initialization

When engine=metalrt in config but models or dylib are missing (e.g.
after brew upgrade), auto-download defaults instead of hard-failing
with "failed to initialize". Falls back to llama.cpp gracefully if
download fails.

Made-with: Cursor

feat: auto-install MetalRT when user switches engine

When a user selects MetalRT via `rcli engine metalrt` or the TUI engine
panel and the dylib is not present, automatically download and install
the MetalRT engine + default models (LFM2.5, Whisper Tiny, Kokoro)
instead of showing an error telling them to run a separate command.

Made-with: Cursor

Update install script for interactive engine choice, update formula for v0.2.3

- install.sh now reads from /dev/tty when piped (curl | bash) so the
  user can interactively choose their engine instead of silently
  defaulting to "Both".
- Supports --engine flag for non-interactive installs:
  curl ... | bash -s -- --engine metalrt
- Update Homebrew formula to v0.2.3 with corrected download sizes.

Made-with: Cursor

feat: download only default models during setup, skip existing, check disk space

- MetalRT setup now downloads only LFM2.5 1.2B + Whisper Tiny + Kokoro
  (~0.9GB). Whisper Small/Medium available on-demand via rcli models.
- Skip already-installed models with green "already installed" message
  instead of re-downloading.
- Check available disk space before downloading; show clear error with
  space required vs available if insufficient.
- Remove hard early-return that blocked adding MetalRT when llama.cpp
  models already existed. Now shows installed engines and proceeds to
  engine choice menu.
- Bump to v0.2.3.

Made-with: Cursor

Update Homebrew formula SHA256 for v0.2.2

Made-with: Cursor

fix: use MetalRT KV cache continuation in TUI text input mode

The TUI text input path was gated on `l_trimmed.empty()`, causing it
to fall back to full-context re-prefill on every turn after the first.
This resulted in TTFT growing linearly with conversation length (e.g.
184ms for 828 tokens). Remove the gate so generate_raw_continue always
uses the KV cache, matching the voice pipeline paths that already
worked correctly. Bumps to v0.2.2.

Made-with: Cursor

Update Homebrew formula SHA256 for v0.2.1

Made-with: Cursor

Bump to v0.2.1: LFM2.5 default, fix install script message

- Default MetalRT LLM changed from Qwen3 0.6B to LFM2.5 1.2B Instruct
- Install script message updated for engine selection flow
- Version bump to 0.2.1

Change default MetalRT LLM to LFM2.5 1.2B Instruct

Made-with: Cursor

Update Homebrew formula for v0.2.0 with MetalRT support

Made-with: Cursor

fix: restore rcli_waveform.gif hero image in README

The MetalRT merge accidentally replaced the animated waveform gif with a
static terminal.png screenshot. Restore the original hero image.

Made-with: Cursor

ci: add CI workflow, fix beta release branch reference

- Add ci.yml: build + smoke test on push to main and PRs
- Fix release-beta.yml: remove hardcoded branch ref (was pointing to deleted feature branch)

Made-with: Cursor

feat: add MetalRT GPU inference engine support (#4)

Add support for MetalRT, a high-performance GPU inference engine for
Apple Silicon. MetalRT provides significant speedups for LLM, STT, and
TTS inference on Metal GPU.

Key changes:
- Dual-engine architecture: llama.cpp (open source) + MetalRT (GPU)
- MetalRT loaded at runtime via dlopen/dlsym (no source dependency)
- Engine selection at setup time (Open Source / MetalRT / Both)
- On-demand MetalRT download from GitHub Releases
- MetalRT LLM models: Qwen3 0.6B/4B, Llama 3.2 3B, LFM2.5 1.2B
- MetalRT STT: Whisper Tiny/Small/Medium (MLX 4-bit)
- MetalRT TTS: Kokoro 82M (bf16, 28 voices)
- Benchmarks: 550 tok/s (Qwen3 0.6B), 8.9ms TTFT
- Full test suite with MetalRT coverage
- Updated README with MetalRT benchmarks and install instructions

MetalRT is proprietary software by RunAnywhere, Inc.
Binary distribution: https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/metalrt-binaries
License: https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/metalrt-binaries/blob/main/LICENSE
Contact: founder@runanywhere.ai

Made-with: Cursor

commit.message: minor changes for gui to make it scrollable and some model fixes

Add multi-turn conversation memory for session-scoped chat

LLM now remembers previous exchanges within a session so follow-up
questions work naturally. History is token-budget-aware and auto-truncates
to fit the context window.

- Add conversation_history to RCLIEngine (API layer) and live_history_ to Orchestrator
- Thread history through all LLM call sites (Tier 1, conversational, Tier 2, live mode)
- Add count_tokens() to LlmEngine for token-budget truncation
- Add rcli_clear_history() API for session reset
- KV cache still used on first turn; subsequent turns use full prompt with history
- Cap at 10 turns (20 entries) with oldest-first eviction

Fix CI build: track src/models/ headers ignored by gitignore

The .gitignore pattern `models/` was matching both the root models/
directory (large binary files) and src/models/ (source headers).
Changed to `/models/` to only match the root directory. Added the
three model registry headers that were missing from git.

Comprehensive feature documentation covering all capabilities:
voice pipeline, 40 macOS actions, model management (9 LLMs,
6 TTS voices, 3 STT engines), RAG with hybrid retrieval,
interactive TUI, benchmarks, and full CLI reference.

- Switch default LLM from Qwen3 0.6B to Liquid LFM2 1.2B Tool (better tool calling)
- Add scripts/package.sh: bundles binary + 8 dylibs with fixed rpaths into portable tarball

- Fix CMakeLists.txt: install rpath before targets, add dylib install rules
- Update release workflow to use package.sh and output SHA256
- Update README with new install instructions, default model, project structure

Made-with: Cursor

Initial commit: RCLI — On-device Voice AI + RAG for macOS

Complete C++17 voice AI pipeline for Apple Silicon:
- STT (Zipformer/Whisper), LLM (Qwen3 via llama.cpp), TTS (Piper/Kokoro)
- 40 macOS actions, hybrid RAG, interactive TUI
- Metal GPU acceleration, 100% local inference

Made-with: Cursor

releases.name: v0.3.3
releases.body:
## Installation

```bash
brew tap RunanywhereAI/rcli https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/RCLI.git
brew install rcli
rcli setup  # download AI models (~1GB, one-time)
```

### SHA256
```
1a7ac50b41422d3cf62739209962a0143e12edc445eb48deca398b8ca9fafc71
```


## What's Changed
* fix: MetalRT segfault crashes, missing dylib packaging, and M5+ forward-compatibility by @shubhammalhotra28 in https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/RCLI/pull/19


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/RCLI/compare/v0.3.2...v0.3.3

releases.name: v0.3.1
releases.body:
## What's New

### MetalRT crash guard (fixes #5)
- **Automatic recovery**: if MetalRT crashes during init (common on some M3/M4 Macs), the next launch automatically falls back to llama.cpp — no more crash loops
- **Helpful error message**: displays a clear message instead of bare "segmentation fault", with instructions to switch engines
- **Defensive guards**: prevents segfault when accessing uninitialized LLM engine in all code paths

### Engine CLI commands
- `rcli metalrt` — launch directly with MetalRT GPU engine
- `rcli llamacpp` — launch directly with llama.cpp engine

### TTFA inline metric
- Time To First Audio now appears inline on the LLM performance line immediately when first audio fires, instead of as a separate line after TTS completes

## Installation

```bash
brew tap RunanywhereAI/rcli https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/RCLI.git
brew install rcli
rcli setup  # download AI models (~1GB, one-time)
```

Or update:
```bash
brew upgrade rcli
```

### SHA256
```
278e0f83d8431957cebb3e2f0020d6049962d961a67a05dd3e855dcd20959596
```

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/RCLI/compare/v0.3.0...v0.3.1

readme_html:
RCLI is an on-device voice AI for macOS. A complete STT + LLM + TTS pipeline running natively on Apple Silicon — 38 macOS actions via voice, local RAG over your documents, sub-200ms end-to-end latency. No cloud, no API keys.

Powered by MetalRT, a proprietary GPU inference engine built by RunAnywhere, Inc. specifically for Apple Silicon.

Demo

Real-time screen recordings on Apple Silicon — no cloud, no edits, no tricks.

Install

[IMPORTANT] Requires macOS 13+ on Apple Silicon. MetalRT engine requires M3 or later. M1/M2 Macs fall back to llama.cpp automatically.

One command:

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RunanywhereAI/RCLI/main/install.sh | bash

Or via Homebrew:

brew tap RunanywhereAI/rcli https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/RCLI.git
brew install rcli
rcli setup          # required — downloads AI models (~1GB, one-time)

Upgrade to latest:

brew update
brew upgrade rcli

If brew install or brew upgrade fails with a checksum error:

# Force-refresh the tap to pick up the latest formula
cd $(brew --repo RunanywhereAI/rcli) && git fetch origin && git reset --hard origin/main
brew reinstall rcli

If that doesn't work, clean re-tap and clear the download cache:

brew untap RunanywhereAI/rcli
rm -rf "$(brew --cache)/downloads/"*rcli*
brew tap RunanywhereAI/rcli https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/RCLI.git
brew install rcli
rcli setup

Quick Start

rcli                             # interactive TUI (push-to-talk + text)
rcli listen                      # continuous voice mode
rcli ask "open Safari"           # one-shot command
rcli ask "play some jazz on Spotify"
rcli metalrt                     # MetalRT GPU engine management
rcli llamacpp                    # llama.cpp engine management

Benchmarks

For More info :

  • https://www.runanywhere.ai/blog/metalrt-fastest-llm-decode-engine-apple-silicon
  • https://www.runanywhere.ai/blog/metalrt-speech-fastest-stt-tts-apple-silicon
  • https://www.runanywhere.ai/blog/fastvoice-on-device-voice-ai-pipeline-apple-silicon

Features

Voice Pipeline

A full STT + LLM + TTS pipeline running on Metal GPU with three concurrent threads:

  • VAD — Silero voice activity detection
  • STT — Zipformer streaming + Whisper / Parakeet offline
  • LLM — Qwen3 / LFM2 / Qwen3.5 with KV cache continuation and Flash Attention
  • TTS — Double-buffered sentence-level synthesis (next sentence renders while current plays)
  • Tool Calling — LLM-native tool call formats (Qwen3, LFM2, etc.)
  • Multi-turn Memory — Sliding window conversation history with token-budget trimming

38 macOS Actions

Control your Mac by voice or text. The LLM routes intent to actions executed locally via AppleScript and shell commands.

Category Examples
Productivity create_note, create_reminder, run_shortcut
Communication send_message, facetime_call
Media play_on_spotify, play_apple_music, play_pause, next_track, set_music_volume
System open_app, quit_app, set_volume, toggle_dark_mode, screenshot, lock_screen
Web search_web, search_youtube, open_url, open_maps

Run rcli actions to see all 38, or toggle them on/off in the TUI Actions panel.

Tip: If tool calling feels unreliable, press X in the TUI to clear the conversation and reset context. With small LLMs, accumulated context can degrade tool-calling accuracy — a fresh context often fixes it.

RAG (Local Document Q&A)

Index local documents, query them by voice. Hybrid vector + BM25 retrieval with ~4ms latency over 5K+ chunks. Supports PDF, DOCX, and plain text.

rcli rag ingest ~/Documents/notes
rcli ask --rag ~/Library/RCLI/index "summarize the project plan"

Interactive TUI

A terminal dashboard with push-to-talk, live hardware monitoring, model management, and an actions browser.

Key Action
SPACE Push-to-talk
M Models — browse, download, hot-swap LLM/STT/TTS
A Actions — browse, enable/disable macOS actions
R RAG — ingest documents
X Clear conversation and reset context
T Toggle tool call trace
ESC Stop / close / quit

MetalRT GPU Engine

MetalRT is a high-performance GPU inference engine built by RunAnywhere, Inc. specifically for Apple Silicon. It delivers the fastest on-device inference for LLM, STT, and TTS — up to 550 tok/s LLM throughput and sub-200ms end-to-end voice latency.

Apple M3 or later required. MetalRT uses Metal 3.1 GPU features available on M3, M3 Pro, M3 Max, M4, and later chips. M1/M2 support is coming soon. On M1/M2, RCLI automatically falls back to the open-source llama.cpp engine.

MetalRT is automatically installed during rcli setup (choose "MetalRT" or "Both"). Or install separately:

rcli metalrt install
rcli metalrt status

Supported models: Qwen3 0.6B, Qwen3 4B, Llama 3.2 3B, LFM2.5 1.2B (LLM) · Whisper Tiny/Small/Medium (STT) · Kokoro 82M with 28 voices (TTS)

MetalRT is distributed under a proprietary license. For licensing inquiries: founder@runanywhere.ai

Supported Models

RCLI supports 20+ models across LLM, STT, TTS, VAD, and embeddings. All run locally on Apple Silicon. Use rcli models to browse, download, or switch.

LLM: LFM2 1.2B (default), LFM2 350M, LFM2.5 1.2B, LFM2 2.6B, Qwen3 0.6B, Qwen3.5 0.8B/2B/4B, Qwen3 4B

STT: Zipformer (streaming), Whisper base.en (offline, default), Parakeet TDT 0.6B (~1.9% WER)

TTS: Piper Lessac/Amy, KittenTTS Nano, Matcha LJSpeech, Kokoro English/Multi-lang

Default install (rcli setup): ~1GB — LFM2 1.2B + Whisper + Piper + Silero VAD + Snowflake embeddings.

rcli models                  # interactive model management
rcli upgrade-llm             # guided LLM upgrade
rcli voices                  # browse and switch TTS voices
rcli cleanup                 # remove unused models

Build from Source

CPU-only build using llama.cpp + sherpa-onnx (no MetalRT):

git clone https://github.com/RunanywhereAI/RCLI.git && cd RCLI
bash scripts/setup.sh
bash scripts/download_models.sh
mkdir -p build && cd build
cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build . -j$(sysctl -n hw.ncpu)
./rcli

All dependencies are vendored or CMake-fetched. Requires CMake 3.15+ and Apple Clang (C++17).

rcli                          Interactive TUI (push-to-talk + text + trace)
rcli listen                   Continuous voice mode
rcli ask <text>               One-shot text command
rcli actions [name]           List actions or show detail
rcli rag ingest <dir>         Index documents for RAG
rcli rag query <text>         Query indexed documents
rcli models [llm|stt|tts]    Manage AI models
rcli voices                   Manage TTS voices
rcli metalrt                  MetalRT GPU engine management
rcli llamacpp                 llama.cpp engine management
rcli setup                    Download default models
rcli info                     Show engine and model info

Options:
  --models <dir>      Models directory (default: ~/Library/RCLI/models)
  --rag <index>       Load RAG index for document-grounded answers
  --gpu-layers <n>    GPU layers for LLM (default: 99 = all)
  --ctx-size <n>      LLM context size (default: 4096)
  --no-speak          Text output only (no TTS)
  --verbose, -v       Debug logs

Contributing

Contributions welcome. See CONTRIBUTING.md for build instructions and how to add new actions, models, or voices.

License

RCLI is open source under the MIT License.

MetalRT is proprietary software by RunAnywhere, Inc., distributed under a separate license.
